page: Microsoft Azure D2s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from a single physical core with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. It includes 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and features a 260 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile characterized by strong single-threaded capabilities but limited multi-core scaling. The server achieves strong results in Geekbench single-core testing and single-threaded gzip compression. However, because the hardware relies on a single physical core, multi-core performance is constrained, as evidenced by a weak Geekbench multi-core score and a poor stress-ng multi-core CPU rating. Memory performance is also a notable bottleneck, with uncached large-block read and write bandwidths falling into the bottom 10% of tested systems.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D2s_v6 offers a low-cost entry point into the dedicated Intel Xeon tier, making it suitable for resource-light workloads that benefit from high clock speeds but do not require high parallel throughput. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, lightweight web serving, basic text processing, and small-scale development or testing environments. It is less optimal for memory-intensive databases, multi-threaded compilation, or heavy parallel computing tasks due to its restricted memory bandwidth and core count.
description: Microsoft Azure D2s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv6 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to a single physical core, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a 260 MB L3 cache, with no local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks highlight strong single-core execution, particularly in Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression tests. However, the single-core architecture limits multi-core scaling, resulting in weak multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks. This server represents a cost-efficient option for workloads that prioritize single-threaded speed over parallel processing. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, basic text processing, and small-scale development or testing environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2s_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C CPU, 2 vCPUs, and 8 GB RAM. It offers strong single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2s_v6 is a general-purpose VM with a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, 2 vCPUs, and 8 GB RAM, optimized for single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dual-vCPU virtual machine delivering strong single-threaded performance and balanced memory for lightweight, cost-conscious general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz with 260 MB L3 cache
2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with hyper-threading
8.0 GB of system memory with no local storage (Premium Storage capable)
Strong single-core performance in compression and synthetic benchmarks
Constrained multi-core scaling and low memory bandwidth
Best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and development environments
